---
title: "Ioannis Savvaidis"
pagetitle: ioaiaaii - Home
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    toc: false
about:
  id: hero-heading
  image: me.webp
  image-shape: rectangle
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:ioannis.a.savvaidis@gmail.com

    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ioannissavvaidis/
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener

    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/ioaiaaii
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener

    - icon: globe
      text: Website
      href: https://ioaiaaii.net
      target: _blank
      rel: noopener
---

:::{#hero-heading}

👋 Hey, welcome to my blog!

I’m a Computer Engineer focused on making complex systems trustworthy. For more than a decade, I’ve been designing and operating infrastructure that remains reliable under pressure — applying the discipline of Site Reliability Engineering to design systems that behave *predictably* at scale.

My work focuses on system performance and observability engineering across on-premises and cloud environments.

I’m also finishing an MSc in Quantum Computing and composing experimental music. These pursuits are less separate than they might appear — each is, at its core, about *understanding* systems deeply enough to *compute* their *dynamics*.

In this blog I share technical notes, experiments, and projects around Site Reliability Engineering, distributed systems, and quantum computing.

:::
